title: JetBrains IDEs
description: Use Claude Code with JetBrains IDEs including IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, and more

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Overview
text:
> Use Claude Code with JetBrains IDEs including IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, and more

Claude Code integrates with JetBrains IDEs through a dedicated plugin, providing features like interactive diff viewing, selection context sharing, and more.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Supported IDEs
text:
The Claude Code plugin works with most JetBrains IDEs, including:

- IntelliJ IDEA
- PyCharm
- Android Studio
- WebStorm
- PhpStorm
- GoLand

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Features
text:
- Quick launch: use `Cmd+Esc` (Mac) or `Ctrl+Esc` (Windows/Linux) to open Claude Code directly from your editor, or click the Claude Code button in the UI
- Diff viewing: code changes can be displayed directly in the IDE diff viewer instead of the terminal
- Selection context: the current selection or tab in the IDE is automatically shared with Claude Code
- File reference shortcuts: use `Cmd+Option+K` (Mac) or `Alt+Ctrl+K` (Linux/Windows) to insert file references such as `@src/auth.ts#L1-99`
- Diagnostic sharing: diagnostic errors from the IDE, such as lint and syntax errors, are automatically shared with Claude as you work
After installing the plugin, you may need to restart your IDE completely for it to take effect.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Installation > Marketplace installation
text:
Find and install the Claude Code plugin from the JetBrains marketplace and restart your IDE.

If you haven't installed Claude Code yet, see the quickstart guide for installation instructions.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Installation
JetBrains IDEs > Usage > From your IDE
text: Run `claude` from your IDE's integrated terminal, and all integration features will be active.
claude
preceding_paragraph: Use the `/ide` command in any external terminal to connect Claude Code to your JetBrains IDE and activate all features:

text: /ide
preceding_paragraph: Use the `/ide` command in any external terminal to connect Claude Code to your JetBrains IDE and activate all features:

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Usage > From external terminals
text:
Use the `/ide` command in any external terminal to connect Claude Code to your JetBrains IDE and activate all features:

If you want Claude to have access to the same files as your IDE, start Claude Code from the same directory as your IDE project root.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Usage
JetBrains IDEs > Configuration > Claude Code settings
text:
Configure IDE integration through Claude Code's settings:

1. Run `claude`
2. Enter the `/config` command
3. Set the diff tool to `auto` to show diffs in the IDE, or `terminal` to keep them in the terminal
For WSL users: Set `wsl -d Ubuntu -- bash -lic "claude"` as your Claude command (replace `Ubuntu` with your WSL distribution name)

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Configuration > Plugin settings > General settings
text:
- Claude command: specify a custom command to run Claude, for example `claude`, `/usr/local/bin/claude`, or `npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code`
- Suppress notification for Claude command not found: skip notifications about not finding the Claude command
- Enable using Option+Enter for multi-line prompts: on macOS only. When enabled, Option+Enter inserts new lines in Claude Code prompts. Disable if the Option key is being captured unexpectedly. Requires a terminal restart.
- Enable automatic updates: automatically check for and install plugin updates, applied on restart

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Configuration > Plugin settings > ESC key configuration
text:
If the ESC key doesn't interrupt Claude Code operations in JetBrains terminals:

1. Go to Settings → Tools → Terminal
2. Either:
  2. Uncheck "Move focus to the editor with Escape", or
  3. Click "Configure terminal keybindings" and delete the "Switch focus to Editor" shortcut
5. Apply the changes

This allows the ESC key to properly interrupt Claude Code operations.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Configuration > Plugin settings
text: Configure the Claude Code plugin by going to Settings → Tools → Claude Code [Beta]:

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Configuration

text: When using JetBrains Remote Development, you must install the plugin in the remote host via Settings → Plugin (Host).

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Special configurations > Remote development
text: The plugin must be installed on the remote host, not on your local client machine.
hostname -I
preceding_paragraph: From inside your WSL shell, run:

text: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Allow WSL2 Internal Traffic" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -RemoteAddress 172.21.0.0/16 -LocalAddress 172.21.0.0/16
preceding_paragraph: Open PowerShell as Administrator and run the following, adjusting the IP range to match your subnet:

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Special configurations > WSL configuration > Allow WSL2 traffic through Windows Firewall
text:
This is the recommended fix because it keeps your existing WSL2 networking mode.

Find your WSL2 IP address

From inside your WSL shell, run:

Note the subnet, for example `172.21.123.45` is in `172.21.0.0/16`.

Create a firewall rule

Open PowerShell as Administrator and run the following, adjusting the IP range to match your subnet:

Restart your IDE and Claude Code

Close and reopen both so the new rule takes effect.

[wsl2]
networkingMode=mirrored
preceding_paragraph: Add this to `.wslconfig` in your Windows user directory:

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Special configurations > WSL configuration > Switch WSL2 to mirrored networking
text:
Mirrored networking requires Windows 11 22H2 or later. If you're on Windows 10, use the firewall rule above instead.

Add this to `.wslconfig` in your Windows user directory:

Then restart WSL with `wsl --shutdown` from PowerShell.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Special configurations > WSL configuration
text: If you're using Claude Code on WSL2 with a JetBrains IDE and see "No available IDEs detected", the cause is usually WSL2's NAT networking or Windows Firewall blocking the connection between WSL2 and the IDE running on the Windows host. WSL1 uses the host's network directly and isn't affected.

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Special configurations
JetBrains IDEs > Troubleshooting > Plugin not working
text:
If the plugin is installed but Claude Code features don't appear in your IDE:

- Ensure you're running Claude Code from the project root directory
- Check that the JetBrains plugin is enabled in the IDE settings
- Completely restart the IDE (you may need to do this multiple times)
- For Remote Development, ensure the plugin is installed in the remote host

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Troubleshooting > IDE not detected
text:
If running `claude` shows "No available IDEs detected":

- Verify the plugin is installed and enabled
- Restart the IDE completely
- Check that you're running Claude Code from the integrated terminal
- For WSL users, see WSL configuration above

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Troubleshooting > Command not found
text:
If clicking the Claude icon shows "command not found":

1. Verify Claude Code is installed by running `claude --version` in a terminal
2. Configure the Claude command path in plugin settings
3. For WSL users, use the WSL command format mentioned in the configuration section

breadcrumb: JetBrains IDEs > Troubleshooting
JetBrains IDEs > Security considerations
text:
When Claude Code runs in a JetBrains IDE with auto-edit permissions enabled, it may be able to modify IDE configuration files that can be automatically executed by your IDE. This may increase the risk of running Claude Code in auto-edit mode and allow bypassing Claude Code's permission prompts for bash execution.

When running in JetBrains IDEs, consider:

- Using manual approval mode for edits
- Taking extra care to ensure Claude is only used with trusted prompts
- Being aware of which files Claude Code has access to modify

For Claude Code installation or login problems outside the IDE, see Troubleshoot installation and login.
quickstart guide
WSL configuration
Troubleshoot installation and login
